name: Linda's Potato Skins
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F.
2. Season potatoes with salt and pepper if desired.
3. Scrub potatoes clean, dry them thoroughly, and pierce several times with a fork.
4. Arrange the potatoes on a baking sheet and bake for about 1 hour or until tender. Let cool slightly before handling.
5. Cut each potato in half lengthwise and scoop out the centers, leaving about an 1/8 inch of potato in the shell. Return shells to refrigerator and chill until ready to use (up to 2 days).
6. If desired, mix together 1/3 cup milk, 1/4 cup butter, salt, and pepper for mashed potato centers.
7. When ready to prepare the skins, melt 1/3 cup butter in a microwave or saucepan.
8. Brush both sides of the potato shells with melted butter.
9. Place cut side up on a baking sheet and bake at 500°F for about 20 minutes or until golden brown and crisp.
10. Distribute bacon, onion, and cheese into each shell. Bake just until the cheese melts.
11. Serve hot. If using large potatoes, you may want to halve them crosswise before serving.
12. This recipe can be doubled if serving multiple people, especially hungry guests.
